summary: Dan Shapiro introduces his woodworking blog with a brief, humorous post explaining that he started serious woodworking about a year prior. He promises to share lessons learned, acknowledging that some have come painlessly while others involved minor injuries. The post's title 'Severed?' is a self-deprecating joke about the risks of power tools.
key_points: Shapiro began serious woodworking approximately a year before this post
He frames learning as a spectrum from easy lessons to painful ones requiring first aid
The post serves as a lighthearted introduction to what would become his blog
money_quotes: I'm learning lots of lessons, some the easy way, some with liberal applications of neosporin.
No, I haven't decided if I'll change the title if I get too frisky with the jointer.
key_insight: This brief introductory post establishes Shapiro's maker identity and self-deprecating humor style that would characterize his later writing.
